message:
fix(robot): gate workflow PolicyEngine with full config (ft-4z7vh)

RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520)
built their PolicyEngine with require_prompt_active hardcoded to
false and without chaining .with_command_gate_config() or
.with_policy_rules(). The resulting engine fed into the workflow's
CxPolicyInjector, so any send_text a workflow step emitted (e.g.,
handle_compaction → "/compact") bypassed three policy surfaces the
direct ft robot send path enforces:

  - operator's safety.require_prompt_active setting
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list

This was a real auth-bypass on the robot-mode mutation surface
found during /security-audit-for-saas (not a duplicate of ft-48ah6,
which was the read/search side).

## Fix

Mirror the construction RobotCommands::Send uses at main.rs:17652
and the non-robot workflow sites at main.rs:21361/21533/25051/27347:

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      config.safety.require_prompt_active,
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning)
  .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone())
  .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone());

Inline comments at both sites cite ft-4z7vh and explain why the
full surface matters. Rate-limit args were already correct, so the
diff is scoped to the third argument + the two builder chains.

## Regression test

robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh
(main.rs tests module):

  - Build a PolicyEngine exactly like the fixed workflow path,
    with require_prompt_active=true.
  - Construct a PolicyInput for ActionKind::SendText on a pane
    whose capabilities say "command running, no prompt active"
    (the PaneCapabilities::running() helper).
  - Assert authorize() returns PolicyDecision::Deny.
  - Pre-fix this engine returned Allow (because it was built with
    require_prompt_active=false); post-fix it denies.

## Verification

rch -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane5-4z7vh-target \
    cargo test -p frankenterm --bin ft \
      robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh

Test binary compilation is currently blocked by pre-existing
concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 "macro select is private" across
multiple unrelated files, E0063 "missing field defer_fts_triggers
in StorageConfig"). These are not caused by this commit and do
not affect the code path being patched. The production fix itself
is verified by structural parity with the five already-green
PolicyEngine::new sites that use the same pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
